name: Google Lens Reverse Image Search
description: Reverse image search via Google Lens. Two modes: text (visible page text) and items (interactive result links/buttons).

browser_automation.description: Uses Google Lens upload-by-URL. Local file uploads are not supported by this workflow — pass a publicly reachable URL.

sequences.name: google_lens_extract
sequences.description: Open Google Lens for an image URL and extract either visible text or enumerated result items.

required_variables.name: image_source
required_variables.description: Publicly accessible image URL (no localhost / file:// — Lens fetches it server-side).

steps.name: Extract result items or visible text

help.summary: Reverse image search via Google Lens (URL-based). Returns visible text or enumerated result items.

parameters.name: image_source
parameters.description: Publicly accessible image URL. Lens fetches it server-side, so localhost/file:// won't work.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: text returns the visible page text (default). items enumerates interactive result links/buttons with text + href + aria-label.

examples.description: Items mode — enumerate clickable Lens result targets.

help.returns: Object with mode and either text (string, truncated to 20k chars) or items (array of {text, href, aria_label}, capped at 100).
notes: Local file uploads aren't supported here — Lens needs a fetchable URL. For a local file, separately host it and pass the URL.
Lens UI is heavily client-rendered; extraction is best-effort and selectors can drift.
